data.title: Airbnb rises after upbeat quarterly results
data.description:
** Airbnb shares rise 13.2% to $159.74 premarket

** Co reports Q4 revenue of $2.48 billion, above estimates of $2.42 billion - LSEG

** Co posts higher revenue as international travel demand remains strong

** Reports quarterly earnings of 73 cents per share, above estimates of 58 cents

** However the company forecast slower growth for Q1 due to tough year-over-year comparisons and a stronger dollar

** It expects its Q1 rev. in a range of $2.23 billion to $2.27 billion, mid-point of which is below analysts' estimates of $2.274 billion

** 10 of 44 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, 28 "hold" and 6 "sell" or lower; their median PT is $150

** Up to last close, ABNB stock which fell 3.5% in 2024, was up 7.4% YTD in 2025

data.title: Serbia Building Permits Rise 6% YoY in December
data.description:
The number of building permits issued in Serbia rose by 6% year-on-year to 2,611 in December 2024.

Out of the total number of permits issued, 82.5% related to buildings and 17.5% to civil engineering construction.

When only buildings are concerned, 82.3% of permits related to residential buildings, and 17.7% to non–residential ones, while regarding civil engineering constructions the largest number related to pipelines, communication and electric power lines (70.9%).

data.title: British equities set for modest weekly gains; higher pound drags
data.description:
Key points:
  • FTSE 100 down 0.3%, FTSE 250 up 0.3%
  • NatWest lost on lacklustre guidance
  • XP Pensions jumped on upbeat revenue forecast

British equities were mixed on Friday, with a stronger sterling hurting export-oriented firms, while encouraging earnings from XP Pensions provided a lift to the midcap index.

The export-heavy FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 slipped by 0.3% at 1047 GMT, after achieving record highs for three consecutive sessions earlier this week, and remained on track to secure a 0.5% gain for the week, marking its third straight weekly increase.

The pound gained in anticipation of a higher UK inflation data for January, due next week, that should cement the consensus expectation for the Bank of England to leave interest rates unchanged in March.

The yield on the UK 10-year benchmark gilt (GB10YT=RR) saw a slight uptick in the session, also adding downward pressure.

British bank NatWest lost 3.7%, after giving a lackluster guidance, further adding to the losses in the benchmark index.

"Guidance lacks a punch," says Matt Britzman, senior equity analyst at Hargreaves Lansdown, adding that the 2025 outlook is in line with expectations, which may cause some weakness in early trading.

Keeping losses at check, Segro gained about 0.2%, after the warehousing group reported a 15% jump in profit in 2024.

British bookmakers Entain and Flutter Entertainment rose 4.5% and 5.4%, respectively, after U.S. peer Draftkings raised its 2025 revenue forecast.

The FTSE 250 (.FTMC) gained 0.3%, boosted from XP Pensions , that jumped 10.7%, their highest since October 16, after an upbeat revenue forecast. The index was set to gain 0.8% this week.

On the flip side, John Wood Group plummeted 32.1%, after the British engineering firm said its free cash flow would be negative this year, instead of positive as previously expected.

Meanwhile, European shares were on track for their eighth straight week of gains.

Among sectoral performers, industrial metals & mining (.FTNMX551020) were the biggest gainer, rising 2.7%, as copper prices climbed to three-month highs.

Precious metals & mining (.FTNMX551030) gained 1.7%. Gold prices rose and were on track for a seventh consecutive weekly gain.

data.title: Commerzbank on Overnight News
data.description:
Commerzbank in its "European Sunrise" note of Friday highlighted:

Markets: 30-year United States Treasuries extend rally after soft bond auction, then consolidate into the close. Two-year USTs better supported in Asia. U.S. stocks close at session high, e-minis grind higher in Asia, Nikkei softer. USD weakens in late New York, stabilizes in Asia, oil little changed.

U.S. Treasury holdings by primary dealers rose to a new record high.

Tariffs: U.S. President Donald Trump has ordered the administration to prepare country-specific reciprocal tariffs; existing non-tariff barriers like subsidies, regulations, lax intellectual property protection and VAT will also be considered (White House factsheet). Howard Lutnick, Trump's pick for commerce secretary, says all studies should be completed by April 1, Trump could start tariffs by April 2. Trump says tariffs on cars will be coming shortly and be "above" the reciprocal tariffs; reiterates threat of 100% tariffs if BRICS play games with the USD.

Geopolitics: Trump says Ukraine will have a seat at the table, won't be meeting Russian President Vladimir Putin in Saudi Arabia next week, would love to have Russia back in G7. Sees China's nuclear arsenal catch up in four-five years, proposes meeting with Russia and China to discuss cutting defence spending in half.

==EUROPE:

Europe: President Emmanuel Macron calls fiscal and monetary framework obsolete, tells FT Europe must "muscle up," respond to Trump's "electroshock," invest in defense, economic and tech capabilities. Trump states the European Union is "very difficult with us," Europe hasn't carried its weight in terms of money, the administration has told Europe and the United Kingdom to buy U.S. military equipment to maintain NATO.

Germany: 13% of voters are still undecided, 18% don't intend to vote or are unsure if they will (ARD survey). Prime Minister Olaf Scholz reiterates call to suspend debt brake, calls for higher defense spending. Latest Allensbach and Infratest polls show CDU/CSU at 32%, Linke at 6% and BSW miss 5% cut-off. Allensbach sees FDP at 5%.

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Intel, Airbnb, DraftKings, GameStop, Roku, Palo Alto, Moderna, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stock futures were mostly higher Friday after President Donald Trump held off on immediately levying reciprocal tariffs on U.S. trading partners.

These stocks were poised to make moves Friday:

Intel was up 3.3% in premarket trading. The chip maker closed Thursday at $24.13, up 7.3%, rising for four straight days and gaining 26% over the period. It was the best four-day stretch for the stock since the four days ended Nov. 2, 1987, when it rose 38%, according to Dow Jones Market Data. The rally this week received a boost Tuesday when Vice President JD Vance said at the AI Action Summit in Paris that the Trump administration "will ensure that the most powerful AI systems are built in the U.S. with American designed and manufactured chips."

Airbnb was profitable in the fourth quarter and the shares rallied 14% even as the short-term stay company forecast revenue in 2025 that was slightly below Wall Street expectations. Airbnb earned 73 cents a share in the fourth quarter, compared with a year-earlier loss of 55 cents, as revenue rose 12% to $2.48 billion and topped estimates. Gross booking value in the period rose 13% to $17.6 billion. Airbnb said it anticipates first-quarter revenue of $2.23 billion to $2.27 billion, below analysts' expectations of $2.29 billion.

Applied Materials, the semiconductor equipment manufacturer, reported fiscal first-quarter adjusted earnings and revenue that beat analysts' expectations but issued mixed guidance, sending the stock down 5.1% in premarket trading. Applied Materials said it expects second-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.12 to $2.48 a share on revenue of $6.7 billion to $7.5 billion, the midpoint of which missed analysts' expectations of $7.21 billion.

Fiscal second-quarter earnings at cybersecurity company Palo Alto Networks were better than Wall Street estimates, but the stock was down 5.6% in premarket trading after Palo Alto estimated third-quarter revenue of between $2.26 billion to $2.29 billion versus forecasts of $2.27 billion, and maintained its full-year outlook for remaining performance obligations.

Coinbase Global reported fourth-quarter earnings of $4.68 a share, easily beating Wall Street forecasts of $2.11. Revenue in the quarter was $2.27 billion, better than estimates of $1.84 billion. Coinbase said trading volume in the period was $439 billion, up from $154 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023. Shares of Coinbase have risen about 40% since Donald Trump's presidential election victory, with the president promising to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world" by ushering in a lighter regulatory regime. The stock was down 1.1% in premarket trading.

DraftKings rose 5% in premarket trading after the online sports betting company reported a wider-than-expected fourth-quarter loss but said it was raising its revenue guidance for 2025 to between $6.3 billion to $6.6 billion, compared with its previous outlook of $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion.

Roku jumped 13%. The streaming company reported a narrower-than-expected fourth-quarter loss and revenue of $1.2 billion that topped analysts' expectations. Streaming households at the end of the quarter were 89.8 million, better than consensus of 89 million. Roku said it sees first-quarter revenue of $1.01 billion, up 14% from a year earlier, and full-year revenue of $4.61 billion. Both forecasts were in line with Wall Street expectations. Roku also said it expects to be operating income positive in 2026.

GameStop rose 6.9% after CNBC reported the video game was considering investing in Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies. The report cited sources familiar with the matter.

Informatica slumped 36% after the provider of cloud-based data management software said it expects first-quarter revenue of $380 million to $400 million, below analysts' expectations of $412 million. For the full year, Informatica expects revenue of $1.67 billion to $1.72 billion, below estimates of $1.78 billion.

Twilio declined 9.1% after the communication-tools provider reported a narrower fourth-quarter loss but issued an outlook that missed expectations. Twilio said it expects first-quarter adjusted earnings of 88 cents to 93 cents a share on revenue of $1.13 billion to $1.14 billion. Analysts had been expecting adjusted earnings of 99 cents a share on revenue of $1.14 billion.

DaVita was down 7.9% after the dialysis provider said it expects adjusted earnings of $10.20 to $11.30 a share in 2025, below analysts' expectations of $11.38.

Earnings reports are expected Friday from Moderna and Enbridge. Vaccine maker Moderna rose 3.1% ahead of its fourth-quarter earnings report.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Correction: Portland General Electric Q4 Net Income Declines; 2025 Outlook Issued
data.description:
(Amends FactSet estimate in the fourth and fifth paragraphs)

Portland General Electric reported Q4 net income Friday of $0.36 per diluted share, down from $0.67 a year earlier.

Four analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.33.

The utility company did not provide quarterly revenue numbers.

Three analysts polled by FactSet expected $663.4 million.

For 2025, the company expects adjusted EPS of $3.13 to $3.33. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $3.21.

data.title: Macedonia Inflation Rate Rises to 16-Month High
data.description:
North Macedonia’s annual inflation rate climbed to 4.9% in January 2025, up from 4.4% in the previous month, marking the highest level since September 2023.

The increase was primarily driven by a sharp rise in transport costs (5.7% vs. 1.6% in December), along with higher prices for housing, water, electricity, gas, and other fuels (1.4% vs. 0.6%).

Inflation also accelerated for furnishings, household equipment, and routine maintenance (3.6% vs. 2.7%) and miscellaneous goods and services (5.9% vs. 4.2%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices edged up 0.2% in January, following a 0.3% increase in the prior month.

data.title: ICE March white sugar delivery totals 411,200 metric tons
data.description:
A total of 411,200 metric tons of mostly Guatemalan white sugar has been tendered against the March contract on ICE Futures Europe, exchange data showed on Friday.

The May contract expired on Thursday.

The total comprised 349,550 tons from Guatemala, 47,600 tons from the United Arab Emirates, 7,050 tons from India and 7,000 tons from El Salvador.

The buyers were Macquarie Bank (261,250 tons) and Marex Financial (149,950 tons).

The sellers were Sucden Financial (328,500 tons), ADM Investor Services (47,600 tons), Louis Dreyfus Company Suisse SA(16,000 tons), Macquarie Bank (12,050 tons) and Marex Financial (7,050 tons).

data.title: Earnings, Geopolitics Limit Wall Street Pre-Bell; Asia Mixed, Europe Up
data.description:
Wall Street futures were modestly lower before the bell Friday, as traders considered Ukrainian peace talks and the unfolding earnings season.

Market denizens await the January national retail sales bulletin, which will be released in Washington at 8:30 a.m. ET, for clues to consumer spending power.

In futures, the S&P 500 fell 0.1%, the Nasdaq was flat and the Dow Jones was off 0.2%.

Asian exchanges traded unevenly overnight, although Hong Kong gained 3.7% in a rally led by tech shares. European bourses edged north at midday on the continent.

Airbnb (ABNB) traded up 13.5% pre-bell after the online lodging broker reported strong Q4 revenue and earnings late Thursday.

Enbridge (ENB), TC Energy (TRP), and Fortis (FTS), among others, plan to report earnings pre-bell.

Also on the economic calendar is the January import and export prices report at 8:30 am ET, followed by the January industrial production release at 9:15 am.

The December business inventories report posts at 10 am, followed by the weekly Baker Hughes domestic oil-and-gas rig count at 1 pm.

Dallas Federal Reserve President Lorie Logan speaks at 3 pm.

In pre-market action, Bitcoin traded at $97,042, West Texas Intermediate crude oil traded higher at $71.49, and 10-year US Treasuries offered 4.53%. Spot gold traded for $2,939 an ounce.

data.title: Gravity Reports Preliminary Unaudited 4Q 2024 Results and Business Updates
data.description:
Seoul, South Korea, Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — GRAVITY Co., Ltd. (NasdaqGM: GRVY) (“Gravity” or “Company”), a developer and publisher of online and mobile games based in South Korea, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2024, prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board and business updates.

PRELIMINARY UNAUDITED FOURTH QUARTER 2024 FINANCIAL RESULTS

Tentative consolidated revenue for the fourth quarter of 2024 is KRW 130 billion, and tentative consolidated operating profit is KRW 16 billion.

The preliminary fourth quarter of 2024 result is mainly attributed by increased revenues from THE RAGNAROK launched in Southeast Asia on October 31, 2024, Ragnarok: Rebirth launched in Taiwan, Hong Kong and Macau on October 31, 2024, and Ragnarok X: Next Generation launched in Japan on November 20, 2024. Tentative consolidated revenue for the fourth quarter of 2024 represented a 1.3% increase in QoQ and a 10.7% decrease in YoY.

Unaudited preliminary consolidated revenue for 2024 is KRW 501 billion, and the operating profit is KRW 85 billion.

The preliminary 2024 figures are unaudited and subject to revision. Final result for the fourth quarter and year ended December 31, 2024 will be provided by our annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 on Form 20-F.

Liquidity

The balance of cash and cash equivalents and short-term financial instruments was KRW 553,202 million as of December 31, 2024.

GRAVITY BUSINESS UPDATES

Two Ragnarok IP-based Games Received ISBN Codes in China

Ragnarok: Back to Glory (Ragnarok: Rebirth, Chinese title: 仙境传说：重生) and PROJECT ABYSS (Chinese title:  仙境传说：初心) have received ISBN codes from the Chinese government on December 24, 2024 and January 21, 2025, respectively.

Ragnarok Online IP-based Games

  • THE RAGNAROK, an MMORPG Mobile and PC game

THE RAGNAROK was officially launched in Southeast Asia on October 31, 2024.

  • Ragnarok in Wonderland, a Casual Healing Tycoon Mobile game

Ragnarok in Wonderland was officially launched in Korea on December 4, 2024.

  • Ragnarok Classic, an MMORPG PC game

Ragnarok Classic was officially launched in Indonesia on December 5, 2024.

  • Ragnarok Begins (Chinese Title: RO 仙境傳說：一定要可愛), an Action Side-Scrolling MMORPG Mobile and PC game

Ragnarok Begins (Chinese Title: RO 仙境傳說：一定要可愛) was officially launched in Taiwan, Hong Kong and Macau on February 13, 2025.

  • Ragnarok M: Classic, an MMORPG Mobile game

Ragnarok M: Classic, a renewal version of Ragnarok M: Eternal Love, is officially launched in Southeast Asia on February 14, 2025, and is underway for its launch in Taiwan, Hong Kong and Macau in the first half of 2025.

  • Ragnarok 3, an MMORPG Mobile and PC game

Ragnarok 3, the new sequel game of Ragnarok Online, is being prepared to be launched in Global within 2026.

  • Ragnarok X: Next Generation, an MMORPG Mobile and PC game

Ragnarok X: Next Generation was officially launched in Japan on November 20, 2024 with preparations underway for its launch in North, Central, and South America and Europe in the first half of 2025.

  • Ragnarok V: Returns, a 3D MMORPG Mobile and PC game

Ragnarok V: Returns will be officially launched in all nations of Southeast Asia in March 2025.

  • Ragnarok M: Eternal Love 2, an MMORPG Mobile and PC Game

Ragnarok M: Eternal Love 2, the next generation new sequel game of Ragnarok M: Eternal Love, is on development.

  • Ragnarok Crush, a Puzzle and Tower Defense Mobile game

Ragnarok Crush will be launching in Global except for China and Japan in the third quarter of 2025.

  • Ragnarok: Back to Glory (Ragnarok: Rebirth), an MMORPG Mobile game

Ragnarok: Back to Glory (Ragnarok: Rebirth) will be re-launched in Southeast Asia and launched in Korea, in the second quarter of 2025.

  • Ragnarok Idle Adventure Plus, an MMORPG Mobile game

Ragnarok Idle Adventure Plus is underway for its launch in Global except for Taiwan, Hong Kong, Macau, China, Korea and Japan in February 2025 and in Taiwan, Hong Kong and Macau in the first half of 2025.

  • Ragnarok Promised Adventure (tentative English title), an MMORPG Mobile game

Ragnarok Promised Adventure (tentative English title) is scheduled to be launched within 2025.

  • Ragnarok Online, an MMORPG PC game

Ragnarok Online is scheduled to be direct-serviced in Latin America in the second quarter of 2025.

  • Ragnarok Landverse, an MMORPG Blockchain and PC game

Ragnarok Landverse Genesis, a global new server onboarding in RONIN platform, will be released in Global in March, 2025.

Ragnarok Landverse will be launched in Vietnam in the first half of 2025 and in Latin America in the second half of 2025. Ragnarok Landverse launching in Vietnam is a PC game without Blockchain.

Other IP-based games

  • TOKYO PSYCHODEMIC, a 2D Cinematic Profiling Adventure PC and Console game

TOKYO PSYCHODEMIC was officially launched in Global on November 28, 2024.

  • KAMiBAKO, a World Craft RPG PC and Console game

KAMiBAKO was officially launched in Global on January 30, 2025.

  • Heroes Gambit, a Strategic Card Battle Mobile game

Heroes Gambit will be launched in Global in the first half of 2025.

  • Scorp Hero, a Character Collecting RPG Mobile game

Scorp Hero is underway for its launch in Japan within 2025.

  • Snow Brothers 2 Special, an Action and Platformer PC and Console game

Snow Brothers 2 Special will be launched in Global in April 2025.

  • Gunbound an MMO Turn-Based Artillery PC game

Gunbound will be launched in Southeast Asia and Latin America in the first half of 2025.

  • Dragonica New Origin, an MMORPG PC game

Dragonica New Origin will be launched in Southeast Asia in May 2025.

Expansion of Other IP business

Gravtiy Co., Ltd. has signed a publishing agreement of Nobunaga's Ambition: The Road to the World (tentative English title), a simulation mobile game based on Nobunaga's IP, in Japan with Kingnet Technology (HK) Limited.

Investor Presentation

Gravity issued an investor presentation. The presentation contains the Company’s recent business updates, results of the fourth quarter in 2024 and Gravity’s business plan. The presentation can be found on the Company’s website under the IR Archives section at https://www.gravity.co.kr/en/ir/updates. Korean and Japanese versions of the presentation are also provided on the website.

About GRAVITY Co., Ltd. ---------------------------------------------------

Gravity is a developer and publisher of online and mobile games. Gravity's principal product, Ragnarok Online, is a popular online game in many regions, including Japan and Taiwan, and is currently commercially offered in 91 regions. For more information about Gravity, please visit http://www.gravity.co.kr.

Forward-Looking Statements:

Certain statements in this press release may include, in addition to historical information, “forward-looking statements” within the meaning of the “safe-harbor” provisions of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act 1995. Forward-looking statements can generally be identified by the use of forward-looking terminology, such as “may,” “will,” “expect,” “intend,” “estimate,” “anticipate,” “believe”, “project,” or “continue” or the negative thereof or other similar words, although not all forward-looking statements contain these words. Investors should consider the information contained in our submissions and filings with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (the “SEC”), including our annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 on Form 20-F, together with such other documents that we may submit to or file with the SEC from time to time, on Form 6-K. The forward-looking statements speak only as of this press release and we assume no duty to update them to reflect new, changing or unanticipated events or circumstances.

data.title: Armlogi Holding Corp. Announces Fiscal 2025 Second Quarter and Six-Month Results
data.description:
• Expanded from 9 to 10 warehouses throughout the first half of fiscal year 2025
  • Total warehouse space increased from 2 million to over 3.5 million square feet
  • Major presence in California, Georgia (Savannah), and Illinois (St. Louis Metro Area)

WALNUT, Calif., Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Armlogi Holding Corp. (“Armlogi” or the “Company”) , a U.S.-based warehousing and logistics service provider that offers a comprehensive package of supply-chain solutions related to warehouse management and order fulfillment, today announced financial results for its fiscal 2025 second quarter and first half ended December 31, 2024. Today, the Company filed its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Financial Results for the Three Months Ending December 31, 2024:

  • Total revenue increased by $9.1 million, or 21.8%, to $51.1 million during the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $42.0 million for the same period in 2023.
    • Revenue from our transportation services increased by $6.2 million, or 20.8%, to $36.1 million during the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared with $29.9 million during the three months ended December 31, 2023, due to the addition of new warehouse locations, which has enabled an increase in shipment volume compared to the same period in 2023. This segment comprises reselling third-party carrier services to our customers.
    • Revenue from our warehousing services increased by $3.1 million, or 25.7%, to $15.0 million during the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared with $11.9 million during the three months ended December 31, 2023, driven by the addition of new warehouses acquired in the last fiscal quarter. This segment comprises inventory management and storage offerings.
    • Revenue from other services decreased by $0.2 million, or 96%. This segment is primarily comprised of customs brokerage services.
  • Costs of sales increased by $16.3 million, or 47.6%, to $50.7 million during the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared with $34.3 million during the same period in 2023. The increase was driven by a rise in freight expenses due to higher UPS shipping charges and increases in lease expenses, employee salary and benefits, and temporary labor costs, as we expanded our warehouse and operations team to support growth.
  • Our freight expenses, lease expenses (primarily warehouse operating lease expenses), temporary labor expenses, warehouse expenses, and salary and benefits increased by $8.3 million, $2.7 million, $2.9 million, $1.2 million, and $0.7 million, respectively, during the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023. The increases in lease expenses were due to the additional operating leases acquired in the last and current fiscal quarter. The increases in freight expenses were due to the increase in UPS expenses. The increases in temporary labor expenses, warehouse expenses, and salary and benefits were due to the expansion of the warehouse operations.
  • Our overall gross profit margin decreased from 18.3% for the three months ended December 31, 2023, to 0.9% for the same period in 2024, primarily due to the increase in the surcharge by UPS and the decreases in customer order volume, as well as some of the recently leased warehouses that are not fully utilized.
  • Our net loss for the three months ended December 31, 2024, was $1.7 million, compared with the net income of $3.7 million for the same period in 2023, representing a decrease of $5.4 million.

Financial Results for the Six Months Ending December 31, 2024:

  • Total revenue increased by $10.4 million, or 12.5%, to $93.6 million during the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $83.2 million for the same period in 2023.
    • Revenue from our transportation services increased by $5.0 million, or 8.3%, to $64.6 million during the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $59.6 million during the six months ended December 31, 2023, due to the addition of new warehouse locations which has enabled an increase in shipment volume compared to the same period in 2023.
    • Revenue from our warehousing services increased by $5.7 million, or 24.7%, to $29.0 million during the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared to $23.2 million during the six months ended December 31, 2023, driven by the addition of new warehouses acquired in the last fiscal quarter.
    • Revenue from other services decreased by $0.4 million, or 93.7%. Other revenue mainly consisted of revenue from our customs brokerage services.
  • Costs of sales increased by $26.4 million, or 37.5%, to $96.7 million during the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared with $70.3 million in the same period in 2023. The increase was driven by a rise in freight expenses due to higher UPS shipping charges and increases in lease expenses, employee salary and benefits, and temporary labor costs as we expanded our warehouse and operations team to support growth.
  • Our freight expenses, lease expenses (primarily warehouse operating lease expenses), temporary labor expenses, warehouse expenses, and salary and benefits increased by $11.5 million, $4.8 million, $5.7 million, $1.8 million and $1.6 million, respectively, during the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023. The increases in lease expenses were due to the additional operating leases acquired in the last and current fiscal quarter. The increases in freight expenses were due to the increase in UPS expenses. The increases in temporary labor expenses, warehouse expenses, and salary and benefits were due to the expansion of the warehouse operations.
  • Our overall gross profit margin decreased from 15.5% for the six months ended December 31, 2023 to 3.3% for the same period in 2024, primarily due to the increase in the surcharge by UPS and the decreases in customer order volume, as well as some of the recently leased warehouses that are not fully utilized.
  • Our net loss for the six months ended December 31, 2024, was $6.3 million, compared with the net income of $6.5 million for the same period in 2023, representing a decrease of $12.8 million.

Liquidity

As of December 31, 2024, we had a balance of cash and restricted cash of $7.4 million, compared with a balance of $10.0 million as of June 30, 2024.

  • Net cash used in operating activities was $9.2 million for the six months ended December 31, 2024, compared to net cash provided by operating activities of $3.5 million for the same period in 2023, representing a $12.8 million decrease in the net cash inflow provided by operating activities.
  • Net cash used in investing activities was $1.0 million for the six months ended December 31, 2024, primarily attributable to $2.1 million cash used for the purchase of property and equipment, $1.0 million cash used for loans extended to others, and $2.0 million proceeds received from loan repayments.
  • Net cash provided from financing activities was $7.7 million for the six months ended December 31, 2024, which was primarily attributable to the net effects of: (i) $0.4 million lent to related parties; (ii) $8.1 million of proceeds from advance payment from the Standby Equity Purchase Agreement (described below).

Operational Highlights

Warehouse Expansion & Facilities

  • Expanded trucking department through increased staffing and equipment to serve major clients, including Amazon
  • Leased a new 60,000 sq ft warehouse in City of Industry, CA, to support trucking operations and partnership with Massimo Group.
  • Opened SAV1 warehouse at Port of Savannah, which quickly became the Company's busiest facility with 70% occupancy
  • Leased 480,000 sq ft warehouse in Ontario, CA, with 46 dock doors and advanced logistics technology

Technology & Operations

  • Incorporated a fleet of electric forklifts across California warehouses as part of the Low Carbon Fuel Standard program
  • Implemented PortPro transportation management software for trucking operations
  • Enhanced warehousing management system to optimize inventory management and warehouse operations
  • Upgraded application programming interface to version 3.5 and integrated with Temu platform, handling over 3,000 orders daily

Financing Arrangements

  • Entered into a $50 million Standby Equity Purchase Agreement (SEPA) with YA II PN, Ltd and up to $21 million in convertible promissory notes, closing two $5 million tranches of pre-paid advances under the SEPA

Management Commentary

Aidy Chou, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Armlogi, commented, “While our significant warehouse expansion and enhanced operational capabilities demonstrate our commitment to long-term growth, we experienced challenges this quarter from increased UPS surcharges and underutilization of our newer facilities. We expect the expansion of our footprint to 3.5 million square feet and our presence in key logistics hubs to position us well for the future, but we intend to focus intently on optimizing our operations and improving facility utilization rates in the near term. Our investments in electric fleets, warehouse management systems, and new transportation partnerships underscore our commitment to sustainable, technology-driven growth. Looking ahead, we anticipate taking decisive steps to address our margin compression while continuing to build the infrastructure needed to serve our growing customer base.”

Conference Call & Audio Webcast

Armlogi’s management team will hold an earnings conference call at 8:00 AM Pacific Time (11:00 AM Eastern Time) on Friday, February 14, 2025, to discuss the Company’s financial results and provide an overview of the Company’s operations. Aidy Chou, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, and Scott Hsu, Chief Financial Officer, will lead the conference call with other company executives available to answer questions.

To access the call by phone, please dial 1-800- 445-7795 (international callers, please dial 1-785-424-1699) approximately 10 minutes before the start of the call. Refer to conference ID: ARMLOGI. **NOTE: THIS CONFERENCE ID WILL BE REQUIRED FOR ENTRY

A live audio conference call webcast will be available online at https://viavid.webcasts.com/starthere.jsp?ei=1707817&tp_key=62a55be146.

About Armlogi Holding Corp.

Armlogi Holding Corp., based in Walnut, CA, is a U.S.-based warehousing and logistics service provider that offers a comprehensive package of supply-chain solutions relating to warehouse management and order fulfillment. The Company caters to cross-border e-commerce merchants looking to establish overseas warehouses in the U.S. market. With eleven warehouses covering over three and a half million square feet, the Company offers comprehensive one-stop warehousing and logistics services. The Company’s warehouses are equipped with facilities and technology for handling and storing large and bulky items. For more information, please visit www.armlogi.com.

data.title: GRANITE CONSTRUCTION INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Granite Construction Incorporated, a leading diversified civil contractor and construction materials producer in the U.S., has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive construction industry. Established in 1922, Granite focuses on infrastructure solutions for both public and private clients, operating through its Construction and Materials segments.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $4,007.6 million, reflecting a 14.2% increase from 2023, driven by higher levels of Committed and Awarded Projects (CAP) and favorable weather conditions.
  • Gross Profit: $572.7 million, representing a 44.5% increase from 2023, primarily due to higher revenue and improved project execution.
  • Operating Income: $207.4 million, a significant increase from $80.1 million in 2023, attributed to increased revenue and effective cost management.
  • Net Income: $126.3 million, up from $43.6 million in 2023, benefiting from improved financial performance and reduced litigation charges.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $2.62, compared to $0.97 in 2023, reflecting the company's enhanced profitability and operational efficiency.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Granite Construction Incorporated, established in 1922, is a leading diversified civil contractor and construction materials producer in the U.S., focusing on infrastructure solutions for public and private clients.
  • Operational Structure: The company operates through two main segments: Construction and Materials. The Construction segment handles infrastructure projects like roads, bridges, and tunnels, while the Materials segment focuses on producing aggregates and asphalt for internal use and third-party sales.
  • Geographical Operations: Granite has vertically integrated operations across several U.S. states, including Alaska, Arizona, California, and others, with additional regional markets in Illinois, Florida, and Texas.
  • Customer Segments: The Construction segment primarily serves public sector clients such as federal and state agencies, while the Materials segment caters to both internal projects and third-party customers like contractors and landscapers.
  • Recent Acquisitions: In 2024, Granite acquired Dickerson & Bowen, Inc., enhancing its presence in central and southern Mississippi, and in 2023, it acquired Lehman-Roberts Company and Memphis Stone & Gravel Company, expanding its operations in the greater Memphis area.
  • Committed and Awarded Projects (CAP): As of December 31, 2024, Granite's CAP stood at $5.3 billion, with significant projects including highway projects in California and a bridge project in Michigan.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth supported by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, which is expected to drive increased project lettings and CAP growth in 2025 and beyond.
  • Sustainability Initiatives: Granite is committed to sustainability, participating in the United Nations Global Compact and focusing on corporate social responsibility, environmental stewardship, and governance.
  • Human Capital: Granite emphasizes employee development and safety, with a focus on inclusion and diversity, aiming to reflect the communities it serves.
  • Safety and Inclusion: Safety is a core value, with ongoing improvements in safety programs, and the company is committed to increasing representation of women and minorities in its workforce.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Acquisitions: The company has undertaken several strategic acquisitions to enhance its market position and operational capabilities. In 2024, it acquired Dickerson & Bowen, Inc., an aggregates, asphalt, and highway construction company, and Granite Infrastructure Canada, Ltd., a construction aggregate producer. These acquisitions are aimed at expanding the company's footprint in key regions and strengthening its materials segment.
  • Operational Reorganization: The company reorganized its operational structure to align more closely with its two reportable segments, Construction and Materials, to better leverage expertise and improve operational efficiency.
  • Capital Management: The company has been actively managing its capital through a combination of debt and equity transactions. In 2024, it repurchased approximately $30.2 million in aggregate principal amount of its 2.75% Convertible Notes, incurring a $27.6 million loss on debt extinguishment. The company also issued convertible notes, raising $373.75 million, and engaged in capped call transactions to mitigate potential dilution. Furthermore, the company repurchased 524,800 shares of its common stock under the Board-approved share repurchase program, with $189.5 million remaining available for future repurchases. The company maintained its quarterly dividend at $0.52 per share, reflecting a commitment to returning value to shareholders.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, the company plans to continue its strategic focus on acquisitions to support growth in its core markets. It anticipates capital expenditures of between $140 million and $160 million in 2025, including strategic investments in materials. The company expects its strong Committed and Awarded Projects (CAP) balance, supported by a favorable public funding environment, to provide opportunities for continued growth. Additionally, the company aims to maintain its liquidity position through prudent capital management, including potential further debt reduction and share repurchases, while exploring new opportunities for strategic investments.

Challenges and Risks

  • Economic and Market Risks: Unfavorable economic conditions, such as inflation, high interest rates, and geopolitical tensions, could adversely affect the company's financial position and operations. The volatility in the global financial system and potential government funding disruptions are significant concerns.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces risks related to fixed price and fixed unit price contracts, which can lead to increased project costs due to factors like inflation and incorrect estimates. Additionally, the reliance on government contracts, which may be modified or terminated, poses a risk to revenue stability.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with government regulations, including environmental, health, and safety laws, is critical. Non-compliance could result in substantial penalties and affect the company's ability to secure future contracts.
  • Emerging Risks: The company is exploring the use of artificial intelligence tools, which presents risks related to data security, regulatory compliance, and potential misuse by employees or third parties.
  • Climate Change Risks: Physical and regulatory risks related to climate change could impact project costs and demand for services. The company is committed to sustainability but acknowledges the challenges in achieving its targets.
  • Management Strategies: Management highlights the importance of strategic growth through acquisitions and divestitures, which involve risks such as integration challenges and potential liabilities. The company is focused on maintaining a strong balance sheet and leveraging its vertically integrated operations to enhance competitiveness.
  • Market Risk Management: Granite Construction Incorporated is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in commodity prices and interest rates. The company uses financial contracts to manage some of these risks but acknowledges that significant price fluctuations could adversely affect its financial condition.

SEC Filing: GRANITE CONSTRUCTION INC [ GVA ] - 10-K - Feb. 13, 2025

data.title: BREAD FINANCIAL HOLDINGS, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Bread Financial Holdings, Inc., a leading provider of credit card and other loan products, has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's efforts to navigate a complex economic environment while continuing to innovate and expand its product offerings.

Financial Highlights

Total net interest and non-interest income: $3,838 million, down 11% from 2023 due to decreased late fees and higher funding costs.

Net income: $277 million, decreased by 61% compared to 2023, impacted by higher credit losses and lower non-interest income.

Net income per diluted share: $5.49, a decrease of 62% from 2023, reflecting the decline in net income.

Adjusted net income: $381 million, reflecting adjustments for repurchased Convertible Notes.

Adjusted net income per diluted share: $7.55, adjusted for the impact of repurchased Convertible Notes.

Income from continuing operations: $279 million, decreased by 62% from 2023, primarily due to lower total net interest and non-interest income.

Income from continuing operations per diluted share: $5.54, reflecting a 62% decrease from 2023.

Adjusted income from continuing operations per diluted share: $7.60, adjusted for the impact of repurchased Convertible Notes.

Net interest margin: 18.3%, down from 19.5% in 2023, primarily due to decreased late fees and higher funding costs.

Return on average tangible common equity: 11.4%, a decrease from 38.0% in 2023, reflecting lower net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates through a single reportable segment, with its primary source of revenue being interest and fees on loans from various credit card and other loan products. Additionally, revenue is generated from contractual relationships with brand partners.

Customer Segments: The company provides products relevant across all customer segments, including Gen Z, Millennials, Gen X, and Baby Boomers. Gen Z and Millennials are more likely to be drawn to cash flow management products, while Gen X and Baby Boomers prefer rewards and convenience.

Sales Units: As of December 31, 2024, the company had $17.4 billion in principal on credit card and other loans from approximately 38 million open and outstanding accounts, with an average balance of $975 for accounts with outstanding balances.

New Product Launches: In 2022, the company launched the Bread Cashback American Express Credit Card, a direct-to-consumer general-purpose cashback credit card. In the fourth quarter of 2023, the Bread Rewards American Express Credit Card was introduced, offering 3% rewards points on specific purchases.

New Production Launches: The company completed the transition of its credit card processing services to Fiserv in 2022, enhancing speed to market and integration of digital technology.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue developing and scaling its direct-to-consumer lending, payment, and saving products, and to explore strategic business opportunities adjacent to its core credit card business in an evolving payments environment.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, the company focused on strengthening its balance sheet by repurchasing $306 million of its outstanding Convertible Notes, reducing debt and dilution risk. Additionally, the company issued $300 million of 9.750% Senior Notes due 2029 to fund the redemption of $400 million of its 7.000% Senior Notes due 2026. The company also engaged in strategic credit tightening and expanded its direct-to-consumer deposit base, which increased to $7.7 billion, representing 43% of total funding.

Capital Management: The company executed a stock repurchase program, acquiring 1.0 million shares for $55 million to offset dilution from employee stock issuances. It maintained a quarterly dividend, paying $43 million in 2024. The company also extended its revolving credit facility maturity to October 2028 and maintained compliance with all debt covenants. The securitization programs were actively managed, with new issuances and adjustments to conduit facilities to optimize funding.

Future Outlook: For 2025, the company plans to maintain its strategic focus on liquidity and capital management, with expectations of flat average credit card and other loans, but higher end-of-period loans due to new business growth. The company anticipates a modest increase in net interest margin and aims to generate positive operating leverage. It also plans to continue its mitigation strategy in response to regulatory changes and expects a net principal loss rate between 8.0% and 8.2%.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: The company faces significant macroeconomic risks, including market conditions, inflation, interest rates, and recessionary pressures, which could adversely affect consumer spending behavior and the company's financial performance. Global political and social events, such as ongoing wars and military conflicts, also pose risks to the business. The company's reliance on unsecured loans makes it vulnerable to customer credit performance, and any increase in delinquency rates could impact financial results. A significant portion of revenue is generated from a limited number of partners, and the loss of any major partner could materially affect the business. The company's concentration in U.S. consumer credit makes it more susceptible to market fluctuations than more diversified companies.

Operational risks include intense competition in the industry, reliance on accurate models for business management, and the potential for fraudulent activities that could harm the company's reputation and financial results. The transition of credit card processing services to outsourcing providers has posed challenges, and failures in data protection and cybersecurity could critically impair operations.

Regulatory risks are significant, with extensive government regulation and supervision potentially impacting business operations. The CFPB's final rule on late fees, if implemented, could significantly reduce revenue. The company is also subject to potential litigation and regulatory actions that could result in fines and penalties.

Management is closely monitoring economic conditions, including inflation and interest rates, and has adjusted credit reserve modeling to reflect an increased probability of a recession. The company is also evaluating strategies to mitigate the impact of the CFPB's final rule on late fees, although the effectiveness of these strategies remains uncertain.

The company is exposed to interest rate fluctuations, which could affect profitability. Changes in market interest rates impact both finance charges and interest expenses, and the company must manage these risks to maintain financial stability. The potential for increased borrowing costs and limited access to capital markets could also affect liquidity and growth opportunities.

SEC Filing: BREAD FINANCIAL HOLDINGS, INC. [ BFH ] - 10-K - Feb. 13, 2025

data.title: Astera Labs, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Astera Labs, Inc., a leading provider of high-speed connectivity solutions for cloud and AI infrastructure, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report details the company's impressive financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a rapidly evolving industry. Astera Labs continues to innovate with its Intelligent Connectivity Platform, addressing the complex connectivity needs of major hyperscalers and AI accelerator vendors.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $396.3 million, increased by 242% compared to the previous year, driven by a 217% increase in overall unit shipments and higher average selling prices.

Gross Profit: $302.7 million, representing a gross margin of 76.4%, up from 68.9% in the prior year, primarily due to a decrease in inventory write-downs.

Operating Loss: $(116.1) million, increased from $(29.5) million in the previous year, largely due to increased stock-based compensation and personnel-related expenses.

Net Loss: $(83.4) million, compared to $(26.3) million in the previous year, with a significant increase in stock-based compensation expenses post-IPO.

Net Loss Per Share: $(0.64), improved from $(0.71) in the previous year, reflecting the impact of increased shares outstanding post-IPO.

Business Highlights

Intelligent Connectivity Platform: Astera Labs has developed an Intelligent Connectivity Platform designed for cloud and AI infrastructure, integrating semiconductor-based, high-speed, mixed-signal connectivity products with a matrix of microcontrollers and sensors, and the COSMOS software suite.

Product Lines: The company offers four product families: Aries PCIe®/CXL® Smart DSP Retimers, Aries PCIe®/CXL® Smart Cable Modules™, Taurus Ethernet Smart Cable Modules™, Leo CXL Memory Connectivity Controllers, and Scorpio Smart Fabric Switches, addressing connectivity challenges in cloud and AI infrastructure.

Customer Segments: Astera Labs serves major hyperscalers, leading AI accelerator vendors, and system OEMs, focusing on providing tailored connectivity solutions to meet the unique requirements of these customers.

Sales Units: The company has shipped millions of devices across all major hyperscalers, indicating a strong demand for its connectivity solutions.

New Product Launches: Since its inception, Astera Labs has introduced first-to-market PCIe, Ethernet, and CXL products, establishing itself as a trusted partner and supplier in the industry.

Research and Development: The company emphasizes a cloud-based approach to R&D, leveraging cloud computing to accelerate product design and development, with dedicated R&D offices in the United States, Canada, India, and Israel.

Manufacturing and Suppliers: Astera Labs employs a fabless manufacturing model, partnering with TSMC for IC fabrication and using Advanced Semiconductor Engineering and Amkor Technologies for assembly, packaging, and testing.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue investing in technology and product innovation to address the next generation of connectivity challenges in AI and cloud infrastructure, with a focus on expanding its product offerings and enhancing its platform capabilities.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Astera Labs, Inc. has focused on expanding its product offerings and enhancing its Intelligent Connectivity Platform, which includes semiconductor-based, high-speed, mixed-signal connectivity products. The company has made significant investments in research and development to support future growth and innovation, particularly in addressing connectivity challenges in cloud and AI infrastructure. The company completed its initial public offering (IPO) in March 2024, which provided substantial capital to support these strategic initiatives.

Capital Management: The company successfully completed its IPO, raising net proceeds of $672.2 million, which significantly bolstered its cash reserves. Astera Labs has also engaged in stock-based compensation plans, including restricted stock units (RSUs) and an employee stock purchase plan (ESPP), to align employee incentives with company performance. The company has not declared any dividends, focusing instead on reinvesting earnings into growth opportunities. Additionally, the company has repurchased some common stock upon employee termination, reflecting a focus on managing equity dilution.

Future Outlook: Astera Labs plans to continue leveraging its strong cash position to fund operations, invest in research and development, and support overall growth. The company anticipates that its current liquidity will be sufficient to meet its operational needs for at least the next 12 months. Future capital requirements will depend on growth rate, sales and marketing expenditures, and potential mergers or acquisitions. The company remains committed to exploring new opportunities for capital investment, with an emphasis on sustainable growth and innovation in its core markets.

Challenges and Risks

Growth and Profitability Risks: The company may not sustain its current growth rate and could face challenges in managing future growth effectively. It has a history of net losses, and if revenue growth does not keep pace with increasing expenses, maintaining profitability could be difficult.

Market and Product Development Risks: The company operates in a rapidly changing industry with evolving standards. Failure to anticipate market trends, develop new products, or penetrate new markets could adversely affect revenue. A significant portion of revenue is dependent on a limited number of end customers, and losing any of these could materially impact operations.

AI Technology and Infrastructure Risks: The adoption and commercialization of AI technology are uncertain. If customers do not continue to invest in AI infrastructure, or if the company fails to keep up with AI developments, it could materially affect business outcomes.

Supply Chain and Manufacturing Risks: The company relies on a limited number of third-party manufacturing partners, primarily located in East Asia, which exposes it to geopolitical risks, trade disputes, and natural disasters. Any disruption in supply could adversely affect the ability to market and sell products.

Regulatory and Political Risks: Changes in political, regulatory, and economic policies, especially concerning trade with China, have reduced product demand and damaged business. Ongoing trade tensions and regulatory changes could further impact operations.

Cybersecurity Risks: Increasing cyber-attacks and data breaches pose significant risks. Any breach could disrupt operations, damage reputation, and result in financial liabilities.

Financial Reporting and Internal Control Risks: The company has identified material weaknesses in its internal control over financial reporting, which could impair its ability to produce accurate financial statements.

Economic and Market Risks: The semiconductor industry is highly cyclical, and fluctuations in supply and demand can impact financial results. Economic conditions, such as inflation and geopolitical developments, could also adversely affect business.

SEC Filing: Astera Labs, Inc. [ ALAB ] - 10-K - Feb. 13, 2025

data.title: Hongkong And Shanghai Hotels Sees FY Loss Attributable HK$900 Million
data.description:
Hongkong and Shanghai Hotels Ltd :

  • SEES FY LOSS ATTRIBUTABLE HK$900 MILLION

  • SEES UNDERLYING LOSS HK$200 MILLION FOR YEAR

  • EXPECTED RESULTS DUE TO INCREASE IN NET FINANCING CHARGES, OTHERS
